description:
Calculate the required X-coordinates for the Main and Nose landing gear of the eCRM-001 transport to satisfy tip-back and static load constraints, then implement them in OpenVSP.

Read the specification file at /home/ga/Desktop/gear_spec.txt. It contains the aircraft's CG limits, ground line, and the mathematical definitions for tip-back angle and nose gear static load.

1. Calculate the exact X-coordinate for the Main Gear (X_main) to achieve exactly a 15.0° tip-back angle at Max Aft CG.
2. Calculate the exact X-coordinate for the Nose Gear (X_nose) to carry exactly 10.0% of the total static weight at Fwd CG.
3. Write your calculated X_main and X_nose values to a report file at /home/ga/Desktop/gear_report.txt.
4. In the open OpenVSP eCRM-001 model, add a new Pod component named 'MainGear'. Set its X_Location to your calculated X_main, Y_Location to 4.0 m, Z_Location to the ground line (-3.0 m), and enable Y-Symmetry.
5. Add another Pod component named 'NoseGear'. Set its X_Location to your calculated X_nose, Y_Location to 0.0 m, Z_Location to -3.0 m, and ensure Y-Symmetry is disabled.
6. Save the modified model to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM001_geared.vsp3.
